title: Subqueries
learning_outcomes: Derive mathematical and programmatic bounds for Scalar subqueries, multi-row subqueries (IN, ANY, ALL), Correlated Subqueries performance implications.

content_sections.title: Subqueries
content_sections.content:
### Logical Query Processing & Execution Optims
SQL is declarative. The database engine parses, optimizes, and compiles SQL queries into physical execution plans.

#### Logical Processing Sequence
The order of processing differs from lexical ordering:
1.  `FROM` + `JOIN` (Load tables and calculate Cartesian product)
2.  `ON` (Filter join conditions)
3.  `WHERE` (Filter row subsets)
4.  `GROUP BY` (Aggregate rows into groups)
5.  `HAVING` (Filter group subsets)
6.  `SELECT` (Project output columns)
7.  `DISTINCT` (Remove duplicate outputs)
8.  `ORDER BY` (Sort output rows)
9.  `LIMIT` (Trunk row outputs)
*Trap:* Referencing select aliases in the `WHERE` clause triggers compilation errors because the projection has not executed yet.

#### Window Functions ROWS vs RANGE
*   `ROWS`: Defines the boundary frame strictly by offset counts (e.g. `ROWS BETWEEN 1 PRECEDING AND CURRENT ROW`).
*   `RANGE`: Defines frame boundaries by value differences based on the sort column (e.g. duplicate sort values are processed as a single frame boundary block).

#### Leftmost Index Prefix Rules
A composite index on `(A, B, C)` can only optimize queries filtering on `A`, `(A, B)`, or `(A, B, C)`. If a query filters on `B` and `C` without filtering on `A`, the index cannot be traversed because sorting is ordered hierarchically by column indices.

### Asymptotic and Spatial Complexity Analysis
We summarize the computational characteristics for Subqueries implementations in the following reference matrix:

| Process/Operation | Worst-Case Time | Average-Case Time | Space Complexity |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| Core Logic Execution | $O(N)$ | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ |
| Memory Operations | $O(N)$ | $O(1)$ | $O(N)$ |

### Interactive Virtualization Configuration

callouts.title: Common Trap in Subqueries
callouts.content: A common mistake when analyzing Subqueries is ignoring latency bounds and boundary edge cases during concurrent executions.
